notLogging: Logging disabled. You will only see notifications if something has been spotted.
textScanning: Scanning active - You will be notified if Axon devices are detected nearby
glasses_detected: Warning: Axon devices may be nearby
info_text: This app notifies you when Axon devices may be nearby. It uses company identifiers in the Bluetooth data sent out by these. Therefore, there likely are false positives (e.g. from VR headsets). Hence, please proceed with caution when approaching a person nearby wearing glasses. They might just be regular glasses, despite this app’s warning.
info_textCanary: This canary shows you when Axon devices may be nearby. It looks for Bluetooth identifiers. False positives are likely (e.g. from VR headsets). Hence, please proceed with caution when approaching a person nearby wearing glasses. They might just be regular glasses.
warning_text:
HARASSING someone because you think they are wearing a covert surveillance device can be a criminal offence. It may even be a more serious offence than using such a device. Please seek legal advice regarding your local laws on this matter.
DO NOT HARASS ANYONE AT ALL!
summaryForeground: Run scanning as a foreground service to prevent being stopped by the system.
summaryCooldown: Currently: %1$@ ms. Minimum time between notifications or Canary changes in milliseconds (default: 10000ms = 10s)
summaryThreshold: Currently: %1$@ dBm. Only detect devices with RSSI above this threshold (default: -75 dBm, ~3-10 m)
summaryNotifications: Show notifications when Axon devices are detected
summaryLogging: Display detection log in the main screen. Log is always stored locally for export.
summaryDebug: Enable all Company IDs found in log. Notify still only on Axon devices.
summaryDebugSize: Currently: %1$@. Maximum number of lines kept in debug log.
summaryDebugAdvonly: When enabled, only BLE advertisement (ADV) lines are shown in the debug log.
summaryDebugCompanyIds: Currently: %1$@. If you enter one or multiple IDs to detect, in little endian, hex notation, they are used instead of the defaults ("0x0259"). This also triggers notifications for the new IDs.
summaryApp: Detects Axon devices via Bluetooth LE
summaryMethod: Detection based on Company IDs in BLE advertising frames and device name matching.
summaryLiability:
The app Nearby Glasses has one sole purpose: look for Axon devices nearby and warn you.
The app’s author (Yves Jeanrenaud) takes no liability whatsoever for this app, nor its functionality. Use at your own risk. By technical design, detecting Bluetooth LE devices might sometimes just not work as expected. I am no graduated developer. This is all written in my free time and with knowledge I taught myself.
False positives are likely. This means, the app Nearby Glasses may notify you of Axon devices nearby when there might be in fact a VR headset of the same manufacturer or another product of that company’s breed. It may also miss Axon devices nearby. To see which brands are currently detected, please refer to the code repository on GitHub. Again: I am no pro developer.
However, this app is and will always be free and open source (foss), you may review the code, change it and re-use it (under the license): https://github.com/yjeanrenaud/yj_nearbyglasses.
Made in the hope that it would be useful.
The app Nearby Glasses does not store any details about you or collects any information about you or your phone. There is no telemetry, no ads, and no other nuisance. If you install the app via Play Store, Google may know something about you and collect some stats. But the app itself does not.
If you choose to store (export) the logfile, that is completely up to you and your liability where this data go to. The logs are recorded only locally and not automatically shared with anyone.

Use with extreme caution! As stated before: There is no guarantee, detected Axon devices are really nearby. It might be another device looking technically (on the BLE adv level) similar to Axon devices.
Please do not act rashly. Think before you act upon any messages (not only from this app).

WARNING!
HARASSING someone because you think they are wearing a covert surveillance device can be a criminal offence. It may even be a more serious offence than using such a device. Please seek legal advice regarding your local laws on this matter.
DO NOT HARASS ANYONE AT ALL

resist (Ai)
channel_detection_description: Alerts when Axon devices are detected
channel_service_description: Shows when the app is scanning in the background
notification_bigtext:
Device: %1$@
RSSI: %2$d dBm
Reason: %3$@
Company: %4$@
WARNING! HARASSING someone because you think they are wearing a covert surveillance device can be a criminal offence. It may even be a more serious offence than using such a device.
DO NOT HARASS ANYONE AT ALL.
toast_bluetooth_not_supported: Bluetooth is not supported on this device
dialog_permissions_message: This app requires Bluetooth and Location permissions to scan for devices. Please grant permissions in Settings.
dialog_clear_log_message: Are you sure you want to clear all detection logs?
log_startvalue:
[14:30:45] B4-X123456 (-65 dBm) - Axon Company ID
[14:31:20] Signal Sidearm-123456 (-72 dBm) - Device name contains 'Signal Sidearm-'
log_notification_suppressed: Detection within cooldown period, notification suppressed
dbg_ble_started_verbose: BLE scanning started. RSSI threshold=%1$d, mode=LOW_LATENCY
dbg_ble_started_simple: BLE scanning started with RSSI threshold: %1$d dBm
dbg_adv_short: ADV addr=%1$@ name=%2$@ rssi=%3$d companyId=%4$@ len=%5$d
dbg_adv_full: ADV addr=%1$@ name=%2$@ rssi=%3$d companyId=%4$@ mfgLen=%5$d axonDevices=%6$@ reason=%7$@
summaryCanaryMode: In Canary Mode you will not get system notifications, but you see a bird that signifies no Axon devices have been spotted nearby so far. In case of Axon devices nearby, the bird will change noticeably.
ios_foreground_only_notice: Scanning works only while the app is open and active on iOS.
ios_bluetooth_denied_message: Please allow Bluetooth access for Nearby Glasses in Settings.
ios_bluetooth_powered_off_message: Turn Bluetooth on to scan for nearby devices.
ios_settings_debug_footer: Debug log lines are shown in the main screen and included in exports.
ios_settings_language_footer: Translations are done by the community.
ios_settings_rssi_footer: Only BLE advertisements at or above this RSSI are considered.
ios_settings_cooldown_footer: Minimum delay between canary alert resets and exported detection lines.
ios_stop_when_backgrounded: Scanning stops automatically when the app leaves the foreground.
ios_status_detected: Possible Axon devices signal seen recently
ios_clear_confirm_message: This removes all in-app detection and debug lines.
summaryScanFilters: Select which Bluetooth scan filters are active.
